name: Multi-Agent Service
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure Container Apps
services.purpose: Supervisor + specialist agent hosting with Dapr sidecar injection

prod.note: 1 vCPU, 2GB per agent, 3 agent containers, min 2 replicas each

enterprise.note: 2 vCPU, 4GB per agent, 5+ agents, multi-region, 3+ replicas

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for supervisor routing + specialist agent reasoning

prod.note: ~2M tokens/day, GPT-4o primary + GPT-4o-mini for routing

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput, guaranteed latency for all agents

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Agent state persistence, conversation history, task coordination

services.name: Dapr (via Container Apps)
services.purpose: Service invocation, pub/sub messaging, state management between agents

dev.note: Built into Container Apps, no extra charge

prod.note: Sidecar injection at no additional cost

enterprise.note: Enterprise features via Container Apps plan

services.name: Azure Service Bus
services.purpose: Reliable async messaging between supervisor and specialist agents

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: API keys, agent credentials, managed identity secrets

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Distributed tracing across agent calls, latency per agent, token usage

services.name: Log Analytics
services.purpose: Centralized logging for all agent containers — KQL queries, alerts

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for the supervisor routing layer — saves 80% on routing decisions
Enable Dapr pub/sub instead of Service Bus for dev — zero cost inter-agent messaging
Use Cosmos DB serverless for dev/staging — pay only for consumed RUs
Scale specialist agents to zero when idle — Container Apps Consumption plan handles this
Implement agent result caching in Cosmos DB to avoid redundant LLM calls
Set per-agent token budgets to prevent runaway multi-turn costs
